english: W. 1. blister caused by burning, p'ol-mig (phol mig), a bad sore, ulcer, abscess, C., W. -- 2. Thgy., a kind of fungus (mould).

lot, p'ya débs-pa (phya 'debs pa) to cast lots Cs.; lot, fortune Cs., p'ya brtág-pa (phya brtag pa) to judge of lots or fortune Cs.; prognostic Sch., p'ya-bzáṅ (phya bzang), -ṅán (-ngan) good, bad fortune or prognostics Cs.; nór-p'ya (nor phya), k'yim-p'ya (khyim phya) prognostics relative to property, family etc., in drawing lots or playing at dice; p'ya (phya) (daṅ (dang)) gyaṅ (gyang) lot (good luck) and blessing, p'ya daṅ gyaṅ °gúg-pa (phya dang gyang 'gug pa) to call forth good luck and blessing, to secure it by enchantment Glr., rgya-nág-gi p'ya-gyaṅ nyáms-pas (rgya nag gi phya gyang nyams pas) as China's fortune and welfare were prejudiced Glr.; p'yá-mk'an (phya mkhan) fortune-teller Cs., but v. also the next article.
1. = rdzá-mk'an (rdza mkhan), potter. -- 2. v. the foregoing.
curtain before a door, Schr. Sch.

1. resp. for lag (lag), hand; bčom-ldan-°dás-kyis p'yag sá-la brdebs (bcom ldan 'das kyis phyag sa la brdebs), Buddha struck with his hand on the earth, Dzl.; p'yag brgyáṅ-ba (phyag brgyang ba), to stretch forth one's hand, Sch.; with la (la) it denotes also the imposition of hands as a holy ceremony, W.: *čag gyaṅ sál-če* (*cag gyang sal ce*). -- 2. bow, compliment, reverence: p'yag dáṅ-po-la (phyag dang po la), whilst making the first bow, Glr.; also compliment in letters: ... la (la) p'yag graṅs-med bčaṅs (phyag grangs med bcangs), with a thousand compliments to... (a Lama even of a higher order concluded his letter to a nobleman with 10 000 compliments to him as the head of the family, and then to the rest according to rank and age in a descending line with 1000, 100 etc.); therefore p'yag byéd-pa (phyag byed pa) (eleg. gyíd-pa (gyid pa); resp. mdzád-pa (mdzad pa), when e.g. a king is addressed by a Lama, Pth.), in Balti *p'yag byá-ča* (*phyag bya ca*), W. gen. *čag p'ul-če* (*cag phul ce*) or čo-če (co ce), resp. jál-čag čo-če* (*jal cag co ce*), to salute, to pay one's respects, with la (la), e.g. ministers waiting on the king, Glr.; *čág-ya yoṅ* (*cag ya yong*), he comes to pay his respects, W.; p'yag daṅ skór-ba byéd-pa (phyag dang skor ba byed pa), to make bows and circumitions, S.g.; with or without a preceding p'yi (phyi) (vulg. °toṅ ('tong)), to take leave, to bid adieu, B. and vulgo (cf. p'yi (phyi) below), *dé-ne čag p'ul yiṅ* (*de ne cag phul ying*), W., so then I shall take my leave now. -- p'yag °tsál-ba (phyag 'tsal ba), pf. btsal (btsal), imp. tsol (tsol), to make a very low reverence, the head almost touching the ground; more at large: gżán-gyi żábs-la mgó-bos p'yag °tsál-ba (gzhan gyi zhabs la mgo bos phyag 'tsal ba), esp. in use before Lamas and kings; in the introductions of books, also, the authors generally address both deities and readers with the phrase: p'yag °tsál-lo (phyag 'tsal lo). -- 3. impurity, dirt(?); v. some of the following compounds and also °p'yág-pa ('phyag pa). -- 4. sometimes for čag (cag). Comp. p'yag-mk'ár (phyag mkhar) resp. for mk'ar-ba (mkhar ba) staff. -- p'yag-k'úr (phyag khur) W. = p'yag-rtén (phyag rten). -- p'yag-goṅ (phyag gong) the back of the hand Cs. -- p'yag-rgyá (phyag rgya) (मुद्रा) 1. resp. for rgya (rgya) (I.) seal; p'yag-rgyás °débs-pa (phyag rgyas 'debs pa) to seal, to confirm by a seal, v. rgya (rgya) I. This meaning is at present hardly any longer known, but only: 2. gesture, the manner in which the hand and fingers are held by Buddha, by stage-players, Lamas or saints etc., when performing religious ceremonies or sorceries; p'yag-rgyás mnán-pa (phyag rgyas mnan pa) to overcome evil spirits by such gesticulations Dom., gról-ba (grol ba) to set them free, by dissolving the charm Pth. There is a great number of these gesticulations. p'yag-rgya-čén-po (phyag rgya chen po) is said to be a figurative designation of the Uma-doctrine. (The other meanings given by Cs. and Sch. are rather uncertain.) -- p'yag-ṅár (phyag ngar) wrist Cs., yet v. ṅar (ngar) I. -- p'yag-ča (phyag ca) Sch. 'wrought by the hand; an implement', resp. for lag-ča (lag ca), v. ča (ca) III. extr.; p'yag-čás (phyag cas) attributes, carried in the hand, in performing religious dances, cf. p'yag-mtsán (phyag mtshan). -- p'yag-čáb (phyag chab) water for washing the hands and the face. -- p'yag-mčód (phyag mchod) Mil. for p'yag daṅ mčód-pa byéd-pa (phyag dang mchod pa byed pa). -- p'yag-snyígs (phyag snyigs) Lexx. = p'yag-dár (phyag dar). -- p'yag-rtágs (phyag rtags) 1. resp. for lag-rtágs (lag rtags) sign of the hand, impression of a blackened finger in the place of a seal. 2. = p'yag-rtén (phyag rten) (?). -- p'yag-<
